student_skills_page.title: My Employability Skills 🚀
student_skills_page.subtitle: These are the practical steps that prepare you for life after school. Track your progress and get verified by your guidance counselor.
student_skills_page.verified_desc: When a counselor "Verifies" a skill, it serves as an official endorsement of your readiness for university or the workplace.
student_skills_page.transition_desc: Students who complete these checks report feeling 40% more confident during their first semester of higher education.
student_skills_page.counselor_review_desc: Once you've self-marked a skill as complete, your assigned counselor will be notified to review it. They may schedule a short meeting to help you finalize your resume or practice interviews.

riasec.title: Interest Discovery
riasec.results_desc: Here's a snapshot of your current interests.

questions.1: I prefer working with tools, machines, or doing physical tasks.
questions.2: I enjoy building, fixing, or assembling things with my hands.
questions.3: I like working outdoors or being physically active.
questions.4: I'm interested in how machines and technology work.
questions.5: I prefer practical, hands-on tasks over theoretical ones.
questions.6: I enjoy figuring out how things work by taking them apart or experimenting.
questions.7: I'm curious about science and enjoy research or analysis.
questions.8: I like solving complex puzzles and brain teasers.
questions.9: I enjoy reading about new discoveries or scientific breakthroughs.
questions.10: I prefer to think through problems carefully before acting.
questions.11: I enjoy creating things — art, music, writing, or design.
questions.12: I like expressing myself through creative projects.
questions.13: I appreciate beautiful or unusual designs and aesthetics.
questions.14: I enjoy performing, whether it's music, drama, or public speaking.
questions.15: I prefer open-ended tasks where I can use my imagination.
questions.16: I like helping people solve their problems or feel better.
questions.17: I like teaching, mentoring, or coaching others.
questions.18: I enjoy working as part of a team rather than alone.
questions.19: I'm good at understanding other people's feelings.
questions.20: I feel energised when I can make a positive difference in someone's life.
questions.21: I like leading a group and making decisions.
questions.22: I enjoy persuading or influencing others.
questions.23: I'm interested in starting projects or businesses.
questions.24: I like taking risks and competing to win.
questions.25: I feel confident speaking up in group settings.
questions.26: I enjoy organizing information, following clear steps, and keeping things tidy.
questions.27: I like working with numbers, data, or spreadsheets.
questions.28: I prefer clear instructions and well-defined tasks.
questions.29: I'm good at paying attention to details and spotting errors.
questions.30: I like creating order and structure in my environment.

Realistic.tagline: The Builder — practical, hands-on, and action-oriented

Investigative.tagline: The Thinker — curious, analytical, and research-driven

Artistic.tagline: The Creator — imaginative, expressive, and original

Social.tagline: The Helper — empathetic, supportive, and people-focused

Enterprising.tagline: The Leader — ambitious, persuasive, and goal-driven

Conventional.tagline: The Organiser — detail-oriented, structured, and reliable

skills.title: Skills Readiness
skills.results_desc: Here's a snapshot of your current skills.

questions.101: I can clearly explain my ideas to others in writing or speaking.
questions.102: I enjoy finding creative solutions to difficult challenges.
questions.103: I am comfortable using new digital tools and software.
questions.104: I work well with others to achieve a common goal.
questions.105: I can easily adjust when plans change suddenly.
questions.106: I am good at active listening and empathy.
questions.107: I like breaking down large problems into smaller steps.
questions.108: I understand how to stay safe and professional online.

Digital Literacy.tagline: Fluency with modern technology and data

Adaptability.tagline: Flexibility and resilience in changing environments

big_five.title: Personality Profile
big_five.subtitle: IPIP-NEO-50 Big Five Assessment

questions.e1: I am the life of the party.
questions.e7: I talk to a lot of different people at parties.
questions.e8: I don't like to draw attention to myself.
questions.e9: I don't mind being the center of attention.
questions.a1: I feel little concern for others.
questions.a5: I am not interested in other people's problems.
questions.a7: I am not really interested in others.
questions.a8: I take time out for others.
questions.a10: I make people feel at ease.
questions.c4: I make a mess of things.
questions.c5: I get chores done right away.
questions.c6: I often forget to put things back in their proper place.
questions.c10: I am exacting in my work.
questions.n2: I am relaxed most of the time.
questions.n7: I change my mood a lot.
questions.o2: I have difficulty understanding abstract ideas.
questions.o4: I am not interested in abstract ideas.
questions.o6: I do not have a good imagination.
questions.o7: I am quick to understand things.
questions.o9: I spend time reflecting on things.

big_five.toast_success_desc: Your Big Five personality profile has been saved.
big_five.toast_error_desc: Failed to save assessment. Please try again.

big_five.title: Personality Profile (Big Five)
big_five.subtitle: IPIP-NEO-50 Big Five Assessment
big_five.footer: Based on the IPIP-NEO-50 assessment • Completed {{date}}

insights.openness: Your profile shows high cognitive flexibility and a love for complex ideas. You thrive in environments that reward innovation and non-traditional thinking.
insights.conscientiousness: You are highly reliable and disciplined. Your strength lies in execution, planning, and maintaining high standards in your work.
insights.extraversion: You draw energy from social interaction. This natural sociability makes you an effective communicator and influencer in team settings.
insights.agreeableness: You have a natural inclination toward cooperation and empathy. You are a 'bridge-builder' who excels in collaborative and person-centered environments.
insights.neuroticism: Your high emotional stability suggests exceptional resilience. You maintain clarity and composure even in high-pressure or unpredictable situations.

insights.Self-Awareness: Your primary strength is self-reflection. You have a keen understanding of how your internal state affects your choices, which is the foundation of authentic leadership.
insights.Self-Management: You possess high self-regulation. You are able to pivot under pressure and maintain a productive focus when others might feel overwhelmed.
insights.Social Awareness: You are highly attuned to the emotional 'frequency' of others. This empathy allows you to navigate diverse groups and understand unspoken dynamics.
insights.Relationship Management: You excel at interpersonal influence. You naturally know how to build bridges, resolve conflicts, and inspire others toward a shared vision.

caas.title: Career Adaptability (CAAS)
caas.footer: Based on Savickas CAAS Scale • Completed {{date}}

insights.Concern: Your high Concern score shows a strong orientation toward your professional future. You are proactive in planning and anticipating next steps.
insights.Control: You show high Control over your career decisions. You feel empowered to make choices and take responsibility for your professional path.
insights.Curiosity: Your high Curiosity indicates a deep drive to explore different options and learn about the world of work without fear of the unknown.
insights.Confidence: Your high Confidence means you believe in your ability to solve problems and overcome the obstacles that come with career changes.

levels.excellent: Excellent — You are highly adaptable to career changes.
levels.good: Good — You have solid career adaptability skills.
levels.developing: Developing — Focus on building your career readiness.
levels.emerging: Emerging — Consider exploring more career development activities.

work_values.title: Work Values (O*NET)
work_values.footer: Based on O*NET Work Values Inventory • Completed {{date}}

insights.achievement: You are primarily motivated by a sense of accomplishment. You thrive in roles that allow you to use your strongest abilities and see visible results of your hard work.
insights.independence: Freedom and autonomy are your core requirements. You are most satisfied when you can plan your own work and make decisions with minimal supervision.
insights.recognition: You value status and potential for advancement. You are driven by environments that offer prestige and clear paths for professional growth and leadership.
insights.relationships: The 'human' aspect of work is vital for you. You find meaning in providing service to others and working in a friendly, non-competitive environment.
insights.support: You value stability and supportive management. You prefer organizations with strong leadership that stands behind its employees and provides a secure future.
insights.working_conditions: You prioritize the environment and structure of the work. You are most productive when your physical surroundings and the logistics of the job are consistent and well-managed.

hero.title: Every student deserves a future they're excited about
hero.subtitle: A comprehensive, school-wide career development program that helps students discover who they are, explore what's possible, and plan their next steps — with support every step of the way.

features.title: A complete guidance ecosystem
features.subtitle: Not just a career test — a developmental, multi-year program that grows with every student.

discovery.title: Self-Discovery
discovery.description: Guided assessments help students understand their interests, strengths, and values through age-appropriate activities.

exploration.title: Career Exploration
exploration.description: Browse career families, real-world roles, pathways, and day-in-the-life stories to spark curiosity.

planning.title: Pathway Planning
planning.description: Map out next steps across university, vocational, apprenticeship, entrepreneurship, and more.

family.title: Family Engagement
family.description: Parent-friendly reports and discussion guides that encourage supportive conversations at home.

counselor.title: Counselor & Admin Tools
counselor.description: Priority dashboards, intervention tracking, meeting logs, and school-wide analytics.

coach.title: AI Career Coach
coach.description: Safe, age-appropriate AI agents that help students explore, reflect, and plan — with human oversight built in.

journey.title: A journey, not a one-time test
journey.subtitle: Career guidance develops over time. Our program grows with each student across their school years.

discovery.title: Discover Myself
discovery.description: Early self-discovery through interests, strengths, and values. Simple activities with no pressure.

exploration.title: Explore Possibilities
exploration.description: Deeper career profiling, subject-choice support, and exposure to the world of work.

planning.title: Plan My Path
planning.description: Pathway planning, application readiness, portfolio building, and transition support.

launch.title: Launch with Confidence

prompts.strengths: Help me reflect on my strengths

report.title: Your Career Interest Profile

report.meaning_desc: You are primarily drawn to {{primary}} and {{secondary}}. There are many exciting paths that combine these interests — and you don't have to choose just one right now.
report.meaning_unknown: We're still learning about your interests! Try taking the assessment again or exploring different career families to see what resonates with you.
report.career_families_desc: These broad areas connect well with your interests.

actions.0: Research a professional whose work involves your top interests
actions.1: Look into a university program related to your interests
actions.2: Talk to your counselor about subject choices

report.disclaimer: This report is for exploration and discussion only. It reflects your current responses and is not a fixed diagnosis or prediction. Your interests, strengths, and goals will evolve over time. Talk to your counselor for more personalized guidance.

discovery.reportInsight: At this stage, it's all about broad exploration. Don't worry about choosing a career yet. Just look for subjects and activities that sound fun and interesting to you.

report_strategic.portrait_desc: A profile connecting your {{primary}} and {{secondary}} interests with your {{capacity}} capacity as you grow.
report_strategic.synergy_desc_base: Your dominant interest in {{primary}} is not an isolated trait; it is powered by your core personality markers.
report_strategic.synergy_desc_high_openness: With high Openness, you don't just follow {{primary}} pathways—you innovate within them.
report_strategic.synergy_desc_disciplined: With disciplined Conscientiousness, you perfect the operational efficiency within {{primary}} domains.
report_strategic.synergy_desc_generic: This combination suggests a natural drive toward professional mastery in your chosen field.
report_strategic.resilience_desc: Your adaptability combined with your {{focus}} creates a strong foundation. You are well-positioned to navigate future changes and collaborative projects.
report_strategic.pathways_desc: Career ideas based on your current exploration data.
report_strategic.networking_desc: Your profile excels in mentorship-driven growth. We recommend finding leaders in the {{primary}} sector to understand the 'hidden curriculum' of your future industry.
report_strategic.appendix_desc: A summary of your responses across all career discovery activities.
report_strategic.market_value_desc: A focus on {{category}} indicates a natural ability to translate your interests into real-world projects.
report_strategic.skills_market_value_desc: Developing your {{category}} skills will help you translate your interests into future success.
report_strategic.rationale_desc: This report brings together all your responses. It looks at what you like (Interests), how you work (Personality), how you plan (Adaptability), and what motivates you (Values).
report_strategic.rationale_synthesis_desc: The exploration roadmap below is generated by connecting your {{primary}} interests with your {{trait}} traits and {{value}} values.
report_strategic.data_required_desc: Complete your RIASEC career assessment to unlock this world-class professional strategy.

synthesis.improve_adaptability: Work with your counselor to improve your 'Career Concern' (planning) score.
synthesis.value_alignment: Research how to satisfy your need for {{value}} in your top career matches.
synthesis.eq_focus: Focus on developing your {{domain}} skills to improve team collaboration.
synthesis.stress_management: Leverage your high emotional stability in high-pressure career simulations.

phase_1.title: Discovery Journey
phase_1.goal: Explore your natural talents and what makes you unique.
tasks: Discover how being a {{persona}} shows up in your favorite school subjects.
Draw or write about 3 careers that sound like 'fun' and check if they match your interests.
Talk to a family member about what they think your biggest strength is.

phase_2.title: Curiosity Building
phase_2.goal: Look at the world of work as a giant playground of possibilities.
tasks: Find one person who works in a {{primaryLabel}} field and ask them one question about their job.
Use the 'Career Explorer' to find a job you've never heard of before.
Join a school club or activity that connects to your top interest areas.

phase_3.title: Skill Seeds
phase_3.goal: Start growing the 'how-to' skills you'll need later.
tasks: Complete one school project where you take the lead on a task you enjoy.
Try a new hobby this month that uses your secondary interest area.
Keep a 'Success Journal' for a week—write down one thing you did well each day.

phase_1.title: Subject Alignment
phase_1.goal: Ensure your school choices match your emerging professional identity.
tasks: Review your top subjects: {{subjects}}. Do they excite you?
Discuss with your counselor how your Big Five traits affect your study habits.
Set one academic goal for this term that connects to your primary interest.

phase_2.title: Exposure & Validation
phase_2.goal: Test your interests against real-world professional stories.
tasks: Research 3 professionals in {{primaryLabel}} fields and look at their 'Pathway' (what they studied).
Watch a 'Day in the Life' video for your top matched career on O*NET.
Participate in a school career fair or talk and ask about 'soft skills'.

phase_3.title: Transition Readiness
phase_3.goal: Prepare for the shift to senior secondary or vocational focus.
tasks: Create a draft CV that focuses on your 'Employability Assets'.
Identify one specific skill (like public speaking or coding) you want to master next year.
Reflect on your CAAS score—which area (Concern, Control, Curiosity, Confidence) can you improve?

phase_1.title: Strategic Selection
phase_1.goal: Finalize your post-school destination and academic focus.
tasks: Match your university or vocational choices against your RIASEC and Work Values profile.
Review your 'Market Value Analysis' in this report with a mentor.
Finalize your choice of higher-level subjects to ensure they meet entry requirements.

phase_2.title: Professional Synthesis
phase_2.goal: Bridge the gap between being a student and becoming a professional.
tasks: Reach out to 2 professionals in {{primaryLabel}} for a 15-minute 'Informational Interview'.
Secure a job-shadowing or internship placement for the next school break.
Optimize your digital presence (Portfolio or LinkedIn) to reflect your Strategic Persona.

phase_3.title: Asset Deployment
phase_3.goal: Launch into your next stage with a clear competitive advantage.
tasks: Perfect your personal statement or application essay using your 'Synergy' analysis.
Complete a 'Capstone Project' that serves as an evidence-piece for your top skill.
Mock-interview with your counselor focusing on your 'Human Capital Advantage'.
